The Shop

Things we make — and seeds from the library.

Below are the boxes and kits we assemble by hand. Individual varieties match our seed library categories; each packet links to its full story and growing notes.

Things we make

Seed Collections

The Founders' Collection

Eight packets of our most beloved heirloom varieties, selected by Estelle and Oliver.

Pressed Flower

The Pressing Kit

Everything needed to begin the practice of pressing — built to last.

Seeds

Browse by type as in the seed library. Purchase packets here; provenance and cultivation notes live on each variety page.

Flower

For the cutting garden and the border

Selected

Tagetes erecta 'Lady Margaret'

Lady Margaret Marigold

Deep amber-saffron blooms of startling complexity, each petal edged in a darker copper that deepens through the season.

$6.50Gentle
Selected

Alcea rosea 'Nigra'

Antwerp Black Hollyhock

Flowers of such deep burgundy they appear nearly black in evening light, rising on stately 8-foot spires above wide, velvety leaves.

$7.00Patient

Helichrysum bracteatum 'Monstrosum'

Immortelle Straw Flower

Papery, sun-bright blooms in shades of amber, saffron, and old rose that hold their color and form for years after drying.

$6.00Moderate

Calendula officinalis 'Resina'

Resina Calendula

Intensely resinous single blooms of deep orange, prized above all others for their medicinal and cosmetic properties.

$5.50Gentle
Vegetable

For the table and the root cellar

Selected

Phaseolus coccineus 'Vermont Scarlet'

Vermont Scarlet Runner

Vivid vermillion flowers cascade down sturdy vines all summer, followed by dramatic purple-black pods and richly flavored beans.

$5.00Gentle

Cucumis sativus 'Cornichon de Paris'

Cornichon de Paris

Crisp, thin-skinned cucumbers, harvest them at two inches for traditional French cornichons or let them run to four for slicing.

$4.50Gentle
Herb

For medicine, kitchen, and fragrance

Echinacea angustifolia

Narrow-leaf Echinacea

The original medicinal coneflower of the American prairie, with narrow petals of soft rose-pink and an intensely resinous, tongue-numbing root.

$9.00Patient
Grain

Ancient grains and heritage cereals

Selected

Zea mays 'Four Corners Blue'

Four Corners Blue Corn

Deep indigo-black kernels of extraordinary nutty sweetness, cultivated for over eight centuries in the canyon lands of the American Southwest.

$8.00Patient
Give the gift

A membership, gifted.

A Correspondent membership makes an exceptional gift for the gardener, the reader, the person who appreciates things made with care. Includes a hand-lettered card from us, mailed separately.

Shop gift memberships — from $45.00
✦

Free shipping

On all orders over $40 within the continental US

✦

Ships within 3 days

Most orders leave the farm within three business days

✦

Seed season

Seed orders ship February through September for best viability

The Marigold Society

A Vermont seed library, pressed-flower atelier, and quarterly journal for those who believe the old ways are worth preserving.

Find Us

18 Old Mill Road
Strafford, Vermont 05072

Write to Us
